assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All 4 slices (1024 chips total) will likely go down simultaneously' and 'Your TPU nodes will be drained and recreated', indicating atomic recreation rather than rolling upgrade

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response mentions all slices going down simultaneously but doesn't specifically explain that maintenance on any single slice triggers restart of all slices in the multislice environment

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Your training job will be terminated/interrupted' and indicates simultaneous impact across all slices

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'TPU v5e slices may not be immediately available post-maintenance' and 'The downtime will likely be 30 minutes to several hours depending on maintenance scope and resource availability'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Force an immediate checkpoint' section with specific command example and emphasizes immediate checkpointing as first preparation step

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response provides specific YAML configuration for restart handling and includes environment variable for resuming from checkpoint, plus mentions configuring job for checkpoint restoration

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer maintenance events

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Coordinate timing' and scheduling around checkpoint intervals but does not specifically discuss maintenance windows as a GKE feature to control when events occur
